chapters.title: Past Experiences
chapters.description: Use the passé composé to talk about things you have done.

lessons.prompt: What is the passé composé of 'manger' (to eat) for 'je'?
lessons.explanation: Passé composé = avoir/être + past participle. 'Manger' uses 'avoir': j'ai mangé.

lessons.prompt: Hier, elle ___ allée au cinéma. (Yesterday, she went to the cinema.)
lessons.explanation: Movement verbs like 'aller' use 'être' in passé composé, not 'avoir'.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'We ate at a restaurant last night'
lessons.correctAnswer: Nous avons mangé au restaurant hier soir
lessons.hint: Use passé composé: 'avons mangé'. 'Hier soir' = last night.

lessons.prompt: Which verb uses 'être' in passé composé?
lessons.explanation: DR MRS VANDERTRAMP verbs use être: Devenir, Revenir, Monter, Rester, Sortir, Venir, Aller, Naître, Descendre, Entrer, Rentrer, Tomber, Retourner, Arriver, Mourir, Partir.

lessons.prompt: Ils ___ visité Paris l'année dernière. (They visited Paris last year.)
lessons.explanation: 'Visiter' uses avoir. 'Ont visité' = they visited.

learningMaterial.title: Past Experiences
learningMaterial.intro: Use the passé composé to talk about things you have done.

concepts.explanation: Passé composé = avoir/être + past participle. 'Manger' uses 'avoir': j'ai mangé.
Movement verbs like 'aller' use 'être' in passé composé, not 'avoir'.

concepts.term: Nous avons mangé au restaurant hier soir
concepts.explanation: Use passé composé: 'avons mangé'. 'Hier soir' = last night.
DR MRS VANDERTRAMP verbs use être: Devenir, Revenir, Monter, Rester, Sortir, Venir, Aller, Naître, Descendre, Entrer, Rentrer, Tomber, Retourner, Arriver, Mourir, Partir.
'Visiter' uses avoir. 'Ont visité' = they visited.

chapters.title: Future Plans
chapters.description: Use the futur simple and futur proche to talk about future events.

lessons.prompt: How do you say 'I am going to travel' using futur proche?
lessons.explanation: Futur proche = aller (conjugated) + infinitive. Simple and very common.

lessons.prompt: Demain, je ___ à Paris. (Tomorrow, I will go to Paris.)
lessons.explanation: 'Je vais aller' = I am going to go (futur proche). Or 'j'irai' in futur simple.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'She will study medicine'
lessons.hint: Futur simple: add endings -ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont to the infinitive.

lessons.prompt: What is the futur simple of 'avoir' for 'nous'?
lessons.explanation: Irregular future stem of avoir = aur-. Add -ons: nous aurons.

lessons.prompt: Quand il ___ grand, il sera médecin. (When he grows up, he will be a doctor.)
lessons.explanation: After 'quand' referring to the future, French uses futur simple (not present like English).

learningMaterial.title: Future Plans
learningMaterial.intro: Use the futur simple and futur proche to talk about future events.

concepts.explanation: Futur proche = aller (conjugated) + infinitive. Simple and very common.
'Je vais aller' = I am going to go (futur proche). Or 'j'irai' in futur simple.
Futur simple: add endings -ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont to the infinitive.
Irregular future stem of avoir = aur-. Add -ons: nous aurons.
After 'quand' referring to the future, French uses futur simple (not present like English).

chapters.title: Expressing Opinions
chapters.description: Share your views and agree or disagree politely in French.

lessons.prompt: Which phrase means 'I think that'?
lessons.explanation: 'Je pense que' = I think that. Also: 'je crois que' (I believe that), 'je trouve que' (I find that).

lessons.prompt: À mon ___, c'est une bonne idée. (In my opinion, it's a good idea.)
lessons.explanation: 'À mon avis' = in my opinion. A very useful phrase for expressing views.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'I agree with you'
How do you say 'I disagree' in French?
lessons.explanation: To negate 'être d'accord', wrap with ne...pas: 'je ne suis pas d'accord'.

lessons.prompt: Selon ___, le film était excellent. (According to him, the film was excellent.)
lessons.explanation: 'Selon lui' = according to him. Selon moi/toi/lui/elle/nous/vous/eux/elles.

learningMaterial.title: Expressing Opinions
learningMaterial.intro: Share your views and agree or disagree politely in French.

concepts.explanation: 'Je pense que' = I think that. Also: 'je crois que' (I believe that), 'je trouve que' (I find that).
'À mon avis' = in my opinion. A very useful phrase for expressing views.
'D'accord' means agreed/OK. 'Avec toi' = with you (informal).
To negate 'être d'accord', wrap with ne...pas: 'je ne suis pas d'accord'.
'Selon lui' = according to him. Selon moi/toi/lui/elle/nous/vous/eux/elles.

chapters.title: Travel & Directions
chapters.description: Navigate cities, ask for directions, and book travel in French.

lessons.prompt: What does 'tournez à gauche' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Gauche' = left, 'droite' = right. 'Tournez' = turn (formal imperative).

lessons.prompt: Le musée est en face de la ___. (The museum is opposite the station.)
lessons.explanation: 'Gare' = train station. 'En face de' = opposite/in front of.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Where is the nearest metro station?'
lessons.correctAnswer: Où est la station de métro la plus proche
lessons.hint: 'La plus proche' = the nearest. 'Où est' = where is.

lessons.prompt: Which word means 'roundabout'?
lessons.explanation: 'Rond-point' = roundabout, 'carrefour' = crossroads, 'feu rouge' = traffic light.

lessons.prompt: Prenez la ___ rue à droite. (Take the second street on the right.)
lessons.explanation: Ordinal numbers: première (1st), deuxième (2nd), troisième (3rd).

learningMaterial.title: Travel & Directions
learningMaterial.intro: Navigate cities, ask for directions, and book travel in French.

concepts.explanation: 'Gauche' = left, 'droite' = right. 'Tournez' = turn (formal imperative).
'Gare' = train station. 'En face de' = opposite/in front of.

concepts.term: Où est la station de métro la plus proche
concepts.explanation: 'La plus proche' = the nearest. 'Où est' = where is.
'Rond-point' = roundabout, 'carrefour' = crossroads, 'feu rouge' = traffic light.
Ordinal numbers: première (1st), deuxième (2nd), troisième (3rd).

chapters.title: Work & Daily Routines
chapters.description: Describe your job and daily schedule using reflexive verbs.

lessons.prompt: What does 'Je me réveille' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Se réveiller' = to wake up. It's a reflexive verb: je me réveille.

lessons.prompt: Elle ___ habille avant de partir. (She gets dressed before leaving.)
lessons.explanation: Reflexive pronoun for elle = se, which becomes s' before a vowel.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'I work from home on Fridays'
lessons.correctAnswer: Je travaille à la maison le vendredi
lessons.hint: 'Travailler' = to work. 'À la maison' = at home. 'Le vendredi' = on Fridays.

lessons.prompt: Which phrase means 'I go to bed'?
lessons.explanation: 'Se coucher' = to go to bed/lie down. 'S'endormir' = to fall asleep.

lessons.prompt: Nous nous ___ les dents matin et soir. (We brush our teeth morning and evening.)
lessons.explanation: 'Se brosser les dents' = to brush one's teeth. 'Nous nous brossons' for nous.

learningMaterial.title: Work & Daily Routines
learningMaterial.intro: Describe your job and daily schedule using reflexive verbs.

concepts.explanation: 'Se réveiller' = to wake up. It's a reflexive verb: je me réveille.
Reflexive pronoun for elle = se, which becomes s' before a vowel.

concepts.term: Je travaille à la maison le vendredi
concepts.explanation: 'Travailler' = to work. 'À la maison' = at home. 'Le vendredi' = on Fridays.
'Se coucher' = to go to bed/lie down. 'S'endormir' = to fall asleep.
'Se brosser les dents' = to brush one's teeth. 'Nous nous brossons' for nous.
